content.stem: In a 3×3 matrix, the first row shows: circle, square, triangle. The second row shows: filled circle, filled square, filled triangle. What should appear in the bottom-right position if the third row starts with: striped circle, striped square, ___?
content.explanation: The matrix follows two rules: columns maintain the same shape (circle, square, triangle) while rows maintain the same pattern (outline, filled, striped). The intersection of the triangle column and striped row must be a striped triangle.

metadata.cognitive_skill: simultaneous relational reasoning across two dimensions

distractor_rationale.reasoning: Students focus only on shape progression, ignoring the fill pattern
Students apply the filled pattern from row 2 instead of the striped pattern from row 3
Students continue the shape pattern from the row instead of maintaining column consistency
